unit.name: Ethical risks, deployment safeguards, and open research problems
unit.description: Surveys safety, bias, truthfulness, and adversarial robustness concerns for deployed NLG systems.
unit.summary: The ethics section argues that as language models improve, alignment with human values becomes more urgent [ts=4332s]. It stresses that models should avoid harmful and toxic content and should be unbiased and fair across demographic groups [ts=4341s]. The lecture uses ChatGPT prompt refusal as a positive example but notes that jailbreak prompting can bypass safeguards and elicit harmful output [ts=4357s] [ts=4371s]. It also warns that large language models may be convincing while factually wrong, so truthfulness is a separate reliability problem from fluency [ts=4401s]. Another risk comes from pretraining data: internet corpora contain harmful stereotypes and biases that models can repeat when prompted, while fully cleaning web-scale data is extremely costly [ts=4457s] [ts=4480s]. Finally, the lecture discusses adversarial triggers and toxic degeneration, concluding that models should not be deployed without safeguards, trigger-word robustness checks, and careful thought about user interaction [ts=4496s] [ts=4571s]. The closing outlook says that despite ChatGPT's strengths, major limitations remain in creativity, deep reasoning, and especially reliable evaluation, leaving NLG as an active and exciting research area [ts=4612s] [ts=4638s].

key_points.text: NLG systems should align with human values by avoiding harmful content, toxicity, and unfair bias across demographic groups.
Jailbreak prompts can bypass safety behavior even when a model usually refuses toxic requests.
Large language models can be confidently untruthful, producing factual errors in persuasive language.
Internet-scale pretraining data can encode harmful stereotypes, and full data cleaning is labor-intensive and nearly infeasible.
Universal adversarial triggers and even innocuous prompts can elicit toxic content from pretrained models.
Before deploying or publishing NLG systems, check that outputs are not harmful and that the model is robust to trigger words and adversarial prompts.

kp_catalog.name: Ethical risks and deployment safeguards for NLG
kp_catalog.description: The safety, bias, truthfulness, and adversarial robustness considerations that should gate deployment of text generators.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because ethical risks and deployment safeguards for NLG clarifies the safety, bias, truthfulness, and adversarial robustness considerations that should gate deployment of text generators, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Reinforcement learning and RLHF for NLG
kp_catalog.description: Training text generators with reward functions, including human-preference reward models layered on top of pretrained systems.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because reinforcement learning and RLHF for NLG synthesizes training text generators with reward functions, including human-preference reward models layered on top of pretrained systems into an end-to-end method or integrated recipe.
